subject: DevOps
title: Rolling Deployments
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Rolling Deployments.

hook.text: You have ten servers running v1. You want v2 on all of them. Shutting down all ten means downtime.

buildup.text: A rolling deployment updates servers one at a time. While one updates, the other nine keep serving traffic.

discovery.text: Health checks verify each server before moving to the next. If a server fails, the rollout pauses.

twist.text: During the rollout, some users hit v1 and some hit v2. Your app must handle both versions running simultaneously.

climax.text: Rolling deployments are the default in Kubernetes. Pods update one by one with built-in health checking.

punchline.text: Update one server at a time. Zero downtime, gradual rollout.

quiz.question: What challenge does a rolling deployment create?
options: Two versions run simultaneously and the app must handle both
All servers must be shut down at the same time
The deployment takes exactly twice as long
